sections.heading: Multi-Cloud Cluster Considerations
sections.body:
If you have a [Configure High Availability and Workload Isolation](create-cluster-multi-region),
your BI Connector connection string may include the cloud provider
type (`.gcp` or `.azure`). In the event of a failover in which nodes
on one cloud provider fail and requests are routed to nodes in another cloud
provider, your BI Connector connection string may change.

To mitigate this hazard, add [Analytics Nodes for Workload Isolation](deploy-analytics-nodes)
to clusters deployed only in one cloud provider and set the BI Connector
[Create a Cluster Page](bic-read-preferences) to **Analytics**.
This ensures that your connection string remains constant.

sections.heading: Connection Tutorials
sections.body:
> **Important:** To transition to and connect with MongoSQL,
> the newest BI connection tool from MongoDB, see
> [Transition from Atlas BI Connector to MongoSQL](sql-interface/transition-bic-to-atlas-sql).

You can connect from the following clients using the
BI Connector for Atlas:

- Create a System DSN

- Connect from Excel

- Connect from Tableau Desktop

- Connect from Qlik Sense

- Connect from MySQL Workbench

- Connect from Power BI Desktop (Windows)

sections.heading: Additional Reference
sections.body:
For more information on the MongoDB Connector for Business
Intelligence, see [MongoDB Connector for BI Manual](bi-connector).
